message:
perf(sync,channel): skip redundant Waker clones via will_wake() guard

Apply a consistent optimization across all sync primitives and channel
types: before cloning a new waker into the waiter list, check whether
the existing waker already points to the same task via `will_wake()`.
This avoids an unnecessary `Waker::clone()` (which typically involves
an Arc refcount increment + vtable call) on every re-poll when the
executor re-uses the same waker — the overwhelmingly common case.

Affected sync primitives:
- mutex.rs: LockFuture and OwnedMutexGuard::lock_owned waiter refresh
  (2 sites)
- rwlock.rs: ReadFuture, WriteFuture, OwnedReadFuture, OwnedWriteFuture
  waiter refresh (4 sites)
- once_cell.rs: WaitInit waiter refresh (1 site)
- semaphore.rs: AcquireFuture and OwnedAcquireFuture waiter refresh
  (2 sites)
- notify.rs: Notified future — uses a three-arm match to handle the
  existing-and-same, existing-and-different, and None cases without
  unconditional clone (1 site)

Affected channel types:
- mpsc.rs: Recv future recv_waker registration — match guard avoids
  clone on re-poll (1 site)
- oneshot.rs: RecvFuture waker registration — same match guard pattern
  (1 site)
- watch.rs: Add new `refresh_waker()` method on WatchInner that updates
  an already-queued waiter's waker in-place without pre-cloning the
  Waker or Arc<AtomicBool>. ChangedFuture now calls refresh_waker()
  first and only falls back to register_waker() with a fresh allocation
  if the waiter was pruned. This eliminates both Waker::clone() and
  Arc::clone() on the common re-poll path.

The pattern is identical everywhere: guard the clone_from() with
`if !existing.will_wake(new_waker)` so we only pay the clone cost
when the executor actually changes the waker between polls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
